title: Set-to box or similar device with wifi protected setup button and visual indicators for displaying each stage of a protected setup process
abstract: The application relates to a station, such as a set-top box, which is operable to communicate wirelessly with an access point. Before such a communication is possible, a wireless connection between the station and the access point has to be established, e.g. through a protected setup sequence, comprising a scan of available wireless channels, the exchange of key messages between the station and the access point, and the installation of a key at the station. It is common that such a station does not have a dedicated physical button for initiating this setup. Users are therefore forced to connect the station to a display device, such as a television or a computer. The present application overcomes this inconvenience by providing a physical button (210) in the station or a remote control (260) by which the setup can be initiated. Furthermore, each stage of the setup procedure is visually indicated at the station by either a text output or a blinking light (250), whereby the blinking frequency is specific for the stage of the setup procedure.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A method comprising: receiving a predetermined input at a station, wherein the predetermined input signals an initiation of a protected setup between the station and an access point; initiating a protected setup process, wherein the protected setup process comprises a plurality of stages; during each respective stage of the plurality of stages of the protected setup process, outputting, at the station, a visual indication associated with the respective stage; determining whether the protected setup process resulted in a successful connection between the station and the access point; and outputting, at the station, a visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises a frequency at which a light indicator associated with the station is blinking, wherein the frequency at which the light indicator is blinking is unique to the respective stage. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: if the protected setup process does not result in a successful connection between the station and the access point, identifying a cause of the unsuccessful connection; and wherein the visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process comprises a sequence of blinking one or more light indicators associated with the station, the sequence being associated with the identified cause of the unsuccessful connection. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the predetermined input comprises a sequence of depressing one or more buttons. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the buttons are physically located at the station. 6. The method of claim 4, wherein the buttons are associated with a remote control unit. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises text output at a display physically located at the station. 8. An apparatus comprising: a first interface configured to be used to receive a predetermined input, wherein the predetermined input signals an initiation of a protected setup with an access point; a module configured to: initiate a protected setup process, wherein the protected setup process comprises a plurality of stages; and determine whether the protected setup process results in a successful connection with the access point; and a second interface configured to be used to: during each respective stage of the plurality of stages of the protected setup process, output a visual indication associated with the respective stage; and output a visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the second interface comprises one or more light indicators, and the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises a frequency at which one or more of the light indicators are blinking, wherein the frequency at which one or more of the light indicators are blinking is unique to the respective stage. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the module is further configured to: if the protected setup process does not result in a successful connection with the access point, identify a cause of the unsuccessful connection; and wherein the second interface comprises one or more light indicators, and the visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process comprises a sequence of blinking one or more of the light indicators, the sequence being associated with the identified cause of the unsuccessful connection. 11. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first interface comprises one or more buttons, and the predetermined input comprises a sequence of depressing one or more of the buttons. 12. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the first interface comprises one or more receivers, and the predetermined input comprises a sequence of depressing one or more buttons associated with a remote control unit, the depressing of the one or more buttons being recognized by the one or more receivers. 13. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the second interface comprises one or more displays, and the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises text. 14. One or more non-transitory computer readable media having instructions operable to cause one or more processors to perform the operations comprising: receiving a predetermined input at a station, wherein the predetermined input signals an initiation of a protected setup between the station and an access point; initiating a protected setup process, wherein the protected setup process comprises a plurality of stages; during each respective stage of the plurality of stages of the protected setup process, outputting, at the station, a visual indication associated with the respective stage; determining whether the protected setup process resulted in a successful connection between the station and the access point; and outputting, at the station, a visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process. 15. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 14, wherein the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises a frequency at which a light indicator associated with the station is blinking, wherein the frequency at which the light indicator is blinking is unique to the respective stage. 16. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 14, wherein the instructions are further operable to cause one or more processors to perform the operations comprising: if the protected setup process does not result in a successful connection between the station and the access point, identifying a cause of the unsuccessful connection; and 

 wherein the visual indication associated with the result of the protected setup process comprises a sequence of blinking one or more light indicators associated with the station, the sequence being associated with the identified cause of the unsuccessful connection. 17. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 14, wherein the predetermined input comprises a sequence of depressing one or more buttons. 18. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 17, wherein the buttons are physically located at the station. 19. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 17, wherein the buttons are associated with a remote control unit. 20. The one or more non-transitory computer-readable media of claim 14, wherein the visual indication associated with each respective stage of the protected setup process comprises text output at a display physically located at the station.
